description: 574 §22.F + §23.B — the transient-arbitration register (Move 4). Single-open across the `transient` AND `right-drawer` layers is arbitrated BY CONSTRUCTION: every transient overlay / right-drawer drawer composes the ONE `TransientController` primitive, which bundles the register/closeOthers/unregister triad into the host lifecycle. Because a governed adopter routes its single-open through the controller, the hand-rolled manual triad for that overlay does not exist to drift (the §13 'generation, not grep, is the anti-drift' shape). This gate locks the catalog's POSITIVE coverage: every `adopters` entry must actually compose `TransientController`. A NEW transient is a discovery step — add it to `adopters` + compose the primitive. (`HoverPreviewHost` is intentionally NOT listed: it is timer-driven, not click-opened, so it does not contend for single-open.)
